text: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
generatorSolution: A square has 4 lines of symmetry. You can fold it in half along: (1) a vertical line down the middle, (2) a horizontal line across the middle, (3) a diagonal line from the top-left corner to the bottom-right corner, and (4) a diagonal line from the top-right corner to the bottom-left corner. Each of these fold lines divides the square into two identical halves, so the answer is 4.
